summary: @wagni_bot/bsc-sdk exfiltrates wallet files, SSH/AWS/npm credentials to hardcoded IP on install
details: The package declares both preinstall and postinstall lifecycle hooks that run postinstall.js during `npm install`. The script recursively walks the user's home directory (up to depth 3) matching wallet-related filenames (id.json, wallet.json, keystore.json, phantom.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt, and similar), and reads classic credential paths including ~/.ssh/, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc (authToken), and.env files. File contents (first ~10000 bytes) are POSTed together with os.hostname() and os.userInfo().username to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The package ships no library code — main points at postinstall.js, description is empty, and the scoped name @wagni_bot/bsc-sdk lures developers looking for a Binance Smart Chain SDK. Installing this package on any machine leaks crypto wallet material and developer credentials to the operator of 107.161.90.180.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
